name: Chicken Stew with Rosemary-Garlic Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: The Herb Companion, Feb.-Mar.
For dumplings:
2 cups flour
1 tablespoon baking powder
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 tablespoon butter, cut into pieces
1 beaten egg
2 teaspoons fresh rosemary, chopped
2/3 cup low-fat milk
Heat the butter in a Dutch oven over medium heat and brown the chicken. Remove the chicken and sauté the onion, celery, and garlic for 5 minutes. Sprinkle with flour and mix well. Add the broth, sugar, pepper, rosemary, bay leaves, and parsley; bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Return the chicken to the pot and spoon sauce over it. Cover and bake in an oven set at 350°F for 30 minutes.
Remove the pan from the oven and increase the heat to 425°F. Remove the bay leaves. Bring the stew to a boil on top of the stove. Shape the dough into sixteen dumplings and drop onto the boiling stew. Return to the oven and cook, uncovered, for 10 minutes. Add the peas, cover, and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
(Consider adding some chopped carrots and mushrooms...)
(Careful if you use dried rosemary instead of fresh - it gets REAL strong REAL quick!)
